Details: Batch studies demonstrated that algal biosorbents were somewhat effective for the removal of fluoride from the aqueous phase, dependent on the species (up to 60 percent for Spirogyra IO2; up to 70 percent effective for Spirogyra IO1), and not effective for others (less than 10 percent effective for Anabaena fertilissima and Chlorococcum humicola, both pretreated with calcium). Removal was shown to be affected by the initial fluoride concentration, pH, adsorbent concentration, and temperature. Greater fluoride removal was observed with: lower initial fluoride concentration, lower pH, and greater adsorbent concentration. The maximum fluoride sorption was reported at 30 degrees C.
